config.title: Köpenick: FAQ
config.subtitle: Getting there, the palace, the fake captain and how to add the Müggelsee.

items.q: How do I get to Köpenick from central Berlin?
items.a: Take the S3 from Alexanderplatz, Ostkreuz or the Stadtbahn stations to Köpenick station, about 20 minutes from Alexanderplatz. From the station, tram 62 or 68 brings you down Bahnhofstraße to Schloßplatz at the old town in a few stops, or you can walk it in about 20 minutes. Everything runs on a normal Berlin AB ticket.

items.q: Is Köpenick worth the trip out of the centre?
items.a: Yes, if you want the Berlin most visitors never see: a real old town on an island, a Baroque palace, a fishermen’s lane and the city’s biggest lake. Plan about two hours for the old town alone, or half a day if you add the palace interior and the little F12 ferry loop.

items.q: What does Schloss Köpenick cost, and when is it closed?
items.a: The Kunstgewerbemuseum inside the palace costs 8 euros, reduced 4. It is closed Monday and Tuesday; in summer it opens Wed-Fri 11:00-17:00 and Sat-Sun 11:00-18:00, in winter Thu-Sun 11:00-17:00. The palace island and garden are free and open even when the museum is not.

items.q: What is the Hauptmann von Köpenick story?
items.a: On 16 October 1906 Wilhelm Voigt, an unemployed shoemaker, put on a second-hand Prussian captain’s uniform, commandeered real soldiers, occupied the Köpenick town hall, arrested the mayor and took around 4,000 marks from the treasury. The stunt became world news and gave German the word Köpenickiade. A bronze statue of the captain by Spartak Babajan has stood at the Rathaus steps since 1996.

items.q: Can you swim in the Müggelsee?
items.a: Yes. The Müggelsee is Berlin’s largest lake at about 7.3 square kilometres, and it has official bathing beaches around its shore. The easiest way in from Köpenick is the S3 to Friedrichshagen, then the walk down Bölschestraße to the lakeside Müggelpark.

items.q: Is the F12 ferry really part of public transport?
items.a: It is. The F12 is a regular BVG ferry line that crosses the Dahme between Wendenschloß and Grünau in about two minutes, and a normal AB ticket covers it. Tram 62 reaches the Wendenschloß side and tram 68 stops by the Grünau pier, so you can ride it as a tiny boat loop from the old town.

items.q: How much time should I plan for Köpenick?
items.a: About two hours covers the old town island, the Rathaus, the waterfront and the palace garden. Add roughly an hour for the palace interior, an hour for the F12 ferry loop, and half a day if you continue to the Müggelsee and the Müggelturm.
